Translation
The Manu, named King Satyavrata, formally saved himself
by tying the small boat of the entire world
to the horn of the Masya Avatara, the Fish Incarnation.
By the grace of the Masya Avatara,
Manu saved himself from the great danger of the flood.
May that same Fish Incarnation
save us from the great and fearful danger
caused by the son of Tvasta.
purā svayambhūrapi svayamāṁ basī
udhīrṇa vātormi ravai karāpā
vātormi ravai karāle
ekora vindhā patitas tatārā
tasmād bhayādena sanostu pārā
In the beginning of creation, a tremendous wind
caused fierce waves of inundating water.
The great waves made such a horrible sound
that Lord Brahma almost fell from his seat
and the lotus into the water of devastation.
But he was saved with the help of
the Lord.
Thus, we also expect the Lord to protect us
from this dangerous condition.

Because all of us think that we are separate and independent Gods.
Purport by Shri Prabhupada,
Here is an explanation of why the conditioned soul cannot see the Supreme Personality of Godhead face to face.
Even though the Lord appears before us as Lord Krishna or Lord Ramachandra
and lives in human society as a leader or king,
the conditioned soul cannot understand Him.
Rascals, mudas deride the Supreme Personality of Godhead,
thinking Him an ordinary human being.
However insignificant we are, we think that we are also God,
that we can create a universe,
that we can create a world,
or that we can create another God.
This is why we cannot see or understand the Supreme Personality of Godhead.
In this regard, Shri Madhvacharya says,
Lingam eva pashyamaha kadachit abhimanastu
devanam apisanniva prayaha kaleshu nastya va
taratam yena
sopitoo
We are all conditioned to various degrees,
but we think we are God.
This is why we cannot understand who God is
or see Him face to face.
This is the sense of the Bhagavad Gita.
The Bhagavad Gita says,

Why misery comes? Because we break these conditions.
